godot_contract_refs.section: Battle-local test entry and isolated process boundary
Headless suite selection, reliable exit codes, and clean-cache testing
P1 aggregate runner, one-command tool, optional root validation, and no-asset completion gate

known_ambiguities: The clean source test has an explicit update and finalize loop, but it does not define Godot SceneTree command-line parsing, process isolation, headless renderer policy, or PowerShell exit-code behavior.

Godot 4.7 retains cyclic GDScript resources when all three inner-class vector scripts are loaded into one runner process. The aggregate suite therefore launches the fixed three single-suite child processes in order and validates each exit code, unique marker, exact check count, and absence of leak diagnostics.

The default battle tool excludes the OpenGL Q0 render test and root Full validation. RepositoryValidation Full is explicit because the existing root validator may require local Platinum assets and renderer-backed checks.

The battle-only P1 aggregate needs no external source or asset roots. The broader PowerShell All selection also reruns the already-verified P0 source audit and therefore retains its explicit analysis and clean-source evidence path parameters.
